text.title: One label, many agents, no self-labelling
text.summary: The same `agent-fixable` label routes to whichever agent in the fleet is free; agents may not apply the label to themselves.

section.title: Fleet routing through one label
section.p: Multiple coding agents share the same backlog: Devin for spec-heavy tickets, Copilot Coding Agent for GitHub-native flows, Codex for one-shot scripts. A single `agent-fixable` label plus a router-side queue keeps the human cognitive load constant — the human says "this ticket is fixable", the router decides which agent runs.

rule.statement: Only one label name (`agent-fixable`) MUST be applied by humans / triage. Vendor-specific labels (`agent:devin`, `agent:copilot`) MAY be added by the router AFTER pickup, never before. Coding agents MUST NOT apply, modify, or remove the `agent-fixable` label on any issue, including those they are currently working on. A branch-protection / repo-policy rule SHOULD enforce this by rejecting label changes from any actor matching `*-bot$` or `agent-*`.
rule.rationale: Self-labelling collapses the gate: if the agent can grant itself the precondition, the precondition is no longer a human decision. Keeping the label vendor-neutral lets the router rebalance load without humans relabelling, and lets a failed pickup in vendor A fall through to vendor B without triage churn.

example.code: # router config on_label: agent-fixable route: - if: { issue.path_match: "infra/**" } then: agent:devin - if: { issue.size: "S" } then: agent:copilot - else: agent:codex post_pickup: add_label: agent:${selected} forbid_label_writes_from: - "*-bot" - "agent-*"
example.note: One human label, deterministic routing, agents cannot tamper.

example.code: # Anti-pattern: agent self-promotes a stale ticket on_no_progress: add_label: agent-fixable # the agent itself
example.note: Agent re-triggers its own pickup, busy-loops, drains tokens.

antipattern.description: Letting humans apply both `agent-fixable` AND `agent:devin` together "to save the router a step".
antipattern.reason: Humans don't track agent availability or vendor outages; pinning the vendor at triage time breaks fleet rebalancing and surfaces incidents as "ticket stuck on Devin queue while Copilot is idle".

section.reference: Gastown discussion: agent-fixable label pipeline pattern.
GitHub Copilot Coding Agent: assign-by-label flow.
